config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary because they are the basic protection against abuse of power. In my view, a healthy society is built by respecting personal dignity, freedom, and due process, not by sacrificing them whenever the majority wants something. From my perspective, collective interests are important, but they should be balanced with strong individual rights, not placed above them by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States we are pretty wary of what happens when the majority or the state gets too much power. Individual rights are the guardrails that stop society from trampling people in the name of the common good, and that is especially important for minorities and unpopular views. At the same time, rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic order do matter, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit. So I would put individual rights first in principle, with narrow exceptions rather than a default of collective priority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should automatically outrank the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how “the common good” can be used to pressure people into silence or conformity. Public order and social harmony matter, but if they are used to excuse censorship, discrimination, or abuse by authorities, then society is not being protected in any meaningful way. To me, a good society is one that protects individual rights first and then limits them only when there is a clear, fair, and necessary reason. The burden should always be on the collective to prove why a right must be restricted, not on the individual to justify why they deserve dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a default rule. From a United States perspective, I am very skeptical of any argument that asks people to trust the collective first, because that is exactly how minorities, dissenters, and unpopular speakers get crushed in the name of order or harmony. Public safety and shared rules absolutely matter, but society has to earn any restriction on rights with a clear, narrow, and fair justification; otherwise, "the common good" becomes a blank check for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual by default, because in Indonesia I have seen how “the common good” can become a convenient excuse for silence, conformity, and abuse. Public order and harmony matter, but they are not meaningful if they are built by suppressing dissent, discrimination, or due process. I do not trust any system that asks individuals to surrender dignity first and promise fairness later. A society is strongest when it protects individual rights firmly and only limits them with clear, necessary, and accountable reasons.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, I do not trust “the common good” when it is used to give the state or the majority a blank check, because that is exactly how unpopular people get silenced and basic freedoms get weakened. Society does have real needs like safety, public health, and order, but those are legitimate only when the limits on rights are narrow, necessary, and accountable. In my view, individual rights should be the default priority, and society has to justify every exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
